explicit_text: My maternal grandfather was a Civil War veteran who served as a drummer and water boy for the Union Army. Though he lived in the South, his family were Northern sympathizers and he escaped conscription into the Confederate Army by fleeing north. He rarely spoke of the war, but I have some letters from him dating to 1918 that mention his experiences.
